summary: Gruber announces a codeless language module he created for BBEdit 8 that provides syntax coloring for Movable Type configuration files. The module supports all documented MT directive keywords plus six undocumented ones found in the default config file. After publication, Phil Ringnalda pointed him to the canonical list of directives in MT's Perl source code, which yielded 25 additional undocumented keywords. Gruber updated the module accordingly and pointedly noted that Six Apart should document these directives.
key_points: The BBEdit 8 language module provides syntax coloring for Movable Type's mt.cfg configuration files
Six undocumented directives were discovered in the default config file beyond what MT's manual listed
The canonical source of truth for MT directives lives in the Perl source code (ConfigMgr.pm), not the documentation
A total of 25 additional undocumented keywords were found in the source, highlighting a significant documentation gap
money_quotes: It provides syntax coloring for comments and MT directive keywords; by default, it claims any file whose name ends with 'mt.cfg'.
Note to Six Apart: It'd be nice to have these documented.
key_insight: When documentation falls short, the source code becomes the only reliable reference — and tools like syntax-coloring modules end up doing the documentation work that the software vendor should have done.
